msg: Building a core long in $RKLB and here's why I'm sizing it up. Neutron reusable medium-lift is finally coming online, Space Systems is now more than half the top line and compounding fast, and a multi-year defense backlog underwrites the next two years. Both launch cadence and margins are inflecting at the same time — this is a conviction holding for me.

Establishing a sizable $CRDO position. As hyperscalers move to 800G and 1.6T, it's the cleanest pure-play on active electrical cables I can find — design wins are ramping at two of the big three, gross margin is pushing past 60%, and there's real optionality in optical DSP on top. Planning to hold this for several quarters at high conviction.

Going to lay out the full $AEHR case because I think it's misunderstood. Burn-in/test for SiC and AI silicon is a multi-year demand wave; test attach rates keep climbing, the old single-customer worry is fading, and margins recover hard in H2. This is a top-conviction core hold for me, full DD to follow.

Putting $HOOD in the book as a platform compounder. The story has broadened well beyond the brokerage — crypto, futures and retirement attach are all stacking — while net interest income cushions the downside. Peers in the cohort are confirming the trend. This earns a full-conviction slot.
Initiating $SOFI for the book @ $58.40. Deposit growth is funding the lending engine and the credit trend keeps surprising to the upside — this is a real franchise, not a trade.

Adding $NBIS to the portfolio ahead of the GPU cloud ramp. The neocloud capacity build-out plus the Nvidia partnership read-through make this a high-conviction position for me — laying out the structured thesis below.
Initiating a 2% position in TMDX as a speculative organ-transport play — keeping it small until the thesis confirms
Nibbling $IOT into Thursday earnings — Samsara has held the 21DMA and I want a little exposure if the print pops. Small, tactical, will size up only if it confirms.
Opened a 3% position in $ALAB via calls — the chart finally looks coiled and ready to break after weeks of chop.
took a quick $RDDT trade off the ad re-rate momentum, keeping it light. just riding the tape here, not a thesis name for me
grabbed some $CRCL on the open at $32.10 — first-day IPOs keep ripping so taking a flyer, tight leash on it though
picked up a starter in $OSCR — decent risk/reward but not betting the farm, sizing it modest while the setup proves out. dyodd
threw a small 1.5% tactical tranche at $PLTR on this flush, just buying the dip not a conviction build
Here's where the book stands today — 21 names open, roughly 92% equities and 8% options. Sharing the running ledger so everyone can see the current mix.
Headline crossing: regulators have accepted $SRPT's gene-therapy filing for an expedited look.

Getting questions on the $AFRM drop. The short report everyone's citing rehashes stuff the Street has chewed on since last year — nothing new and the substance is thin. My position stays on; not adding, not selling.
Keeping $NET on the radar — it's drifting back toward the long-term trendline around $95 and the chart needs to carve out a base before I'd touch it. Flagging the level, haven't bought anything.
noting the HBM remarks on today's call — could matter for $MU down the road. just putting it on people's radar, no action from me
this read-through is a positive for $AVGO holders imo — sharing as background, nothing actionable
$ANET and $VRT are both on my add-more list — waiting for a pullback to build them out. Not doing anything today, just sharing the wishlist.
Month-end book check-in: holding 19 names right now. Just the routine snapshot, nothing to do here.
Rough tape again — $SPY and $QQQ rolled over midday and closed weak. Rate jitters keep capping any bounce we get.
